title: Airbnb Q2 2022 Earnings Results
text: Airbnb beat Wall Street estimates for earnings and posted revenue that was in line with estimates for the second quarter. Earnings per share: $0.56 vs. $0.43 expected by analysts. Revenue: $2.10 billion vs. $2.11 billion expected by analysts. Revenue jumped 58% year-over-year to $2.1 billion helping to drive the company's most profitable second quarter to date. Gross booking value, which Airbnb uses to track host earnings, service fees, cleaning fees and taxes, totaled $17 billion in the second quarter, up 27% year over year. The company said it broke a single-day revenue record on July 4, which it says signals a strong summer season ahead. The company also announced a $2 billion share buyback program. Shares were down about 9% after hours, despite what appeared to be a strong report, suggesting Wall Street was looking for faster growth and a revenue beat.
